metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: AIRR / Authentication / Keycloak (External User)
Technical Action Required: Yes (local Keycloak account creation)
Escalation Required: Yes (AIRR team)
Urgency: Medium (invitation expiry deadline)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User has been invited to join the “UKAEA Early Adopters” project on AIRR and requests creation of a local Keycloak account.

Key points:
- User is external (UKAEA email).
- Invitation link has an expiry date.
- “Local Keycloak entry” indicates use of the documented “without MyAccessID (last resort)” workflow.
- Account must be created in Keycloak before the user can accept the AIRR invitation via “Sign in with Keycloak”.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Confirm invitation context (internal)
   - Verify invitation exists for the user email.
   - Confirm project name: “UKAEA Early Adopters”.
   - Confirm partition/access level requested.

2) Escalate to AIRR team (if not directly handled by HPC Support)
   - Forward ticket to AIRR administrators.
   - Include:
       • Full name
       • Organisation
       • Email
       • Project/partition name
   - Mark urgency if invitation expiry is near.

3) Create local Keycloak account (AIRR/admin action)
   - Create account using provided email.
   - Trigger password setup email.
   - Ensure account is linked to the appropriate realm.

4) Notify user once action is underway
   - Explain that once the local account has been created, they will receive a password setup email.
   - Advise checking spam/junk folders.
   - Instruct them to:
       a) Set password,
       b) Visit invitation link,
       c) Choose “Sign in with Keycloak”,
       d) Use newly created credentials.

5) Provide documentation link
   - Reference official guidance for “Accessing without MyAccessID (last resort)”.

6) Do NOT:
   - Manually approve the project membership unless that is part of the AIRR workflow.
   - Ask for passwords.
   - Override the invitation process.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Has the invitation expired?
- Have you received the password setup email?
- Are you able to access the invitation link successfully?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: UKAEA Early Adopters – AIRR Access

Hi Waqar,

We will arrange creation of the local Keycloak account needed for this AIRR invitation.

Once the account has been created, you should receive an email to set your password. After that, please visit the invitation link, select “Sign in with Keycloak”, and log in using those credentials.

For reference, please see:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/pvc.html#accessing-without-myaccessid-last-resort

Please let us know if you encounter any issues.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not transmit passwords via email.
- Do not request sensitive credentials.
- Ensure external accounts are created only through the approved Keycloak workflow.
- Respect invitation expiry deadlines but do not bypass approval controls.

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Slurm / GPU Scheduling / Walltime Limits
Technical Action Required: No (policy explanation + workflow guidance)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium (jobs being cancelled due to time limit)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is concerned that GPU jobs cannot exceed the 36-hour walltime limit and asks how to make best use of the allocation.

Key principles:
- GPU partitions enforce a maximum walltime limit (e.g. 36 hours).
- This is a scheduler policy limit, not a configuration error.
- The limit applies regardless of remaining budgeted GPU hours.
- Allocated GPU hours (account balance) are separate from maximum walltime per job.
- Long-running workflows should use checkpointing and job chaining.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Confirm policy limit
   - Explain that the 36-hour walltime is an enforced scheduler limit.
   - It cannot be overridden per job at the user level.

2) Clarify allocation model
   - GPU hour budget defines total consumption allowed.
   - Walltime limit defines maximum duration of a single job.
   - Having remaining hours does not permit exceeding the per-job limit.

3) Recommend checkpointing (best practice)
   - Modify code to periodically write restart/checkpoint files.
   - Submit jobs so that they resume from the last checkpoint.
   - Use job arrays or chained submissions to continue computation.

4) Optional: suggest job chaining
   - Use dependent jobs:
       sbatch --dependency=afterok:<jobid> next_job.sh
   - Or include automatic resubmission logic if checkpoint detected.

6) Do NOT:
   - Promise walltime extensions.
   - Modify partition policy.
   - Suggest splitting allocation across multiple concurrent long jobs to bypass limits.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (IF NEEDED)
- Which partition/account are you using?
- How long does your full workflow need to run?
- Does your code support checkpoint/restart?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: Support for job submissions

Hi Sam,

The 36-hour walltime limit is a scheduler policy on the GPU partition and cannot be extended for individual jobs. Even if you have sufficient GPU hours remaining in your allocation, the maximum runtime per job is still capped.

To run longer workflows, we recommend implementing checkpointing so that your job can resume from the last saved state once the walltime is reached. You can then submit follow-up jobs to continue from the checkpoint.

Please let us know if you would like guidance on setting up checkpointing or job dependencies.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Walltime limits are enforced at scheduler level.
- Allocation balance does not override per-job limits.
- Avoid suggesting policy circumvention.
- Promote checkpoint/restart as standard HPC best practice.

So I don’t think I can make max use of my allocation. Are you able to advice what I can do for this?

Many thanks
Sam

benchmark_annotation.summary: Q about how to request CPUs on Wilkes

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Slurm / GPU-CPU Resource Ratios / Wilkes3 (Ampere)
Technical Action Required: No (scheduler policy clarification)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is running on Wilkes3 (Ampere) requesting:
- 1 node
- 1 GPU
- 1 task
- Wants to increase CPUs from 32 to 76 while still using only 1 GPU.

On Wilkes3 Ampere nodes, Slurm enforces a CPU-per-GPU ratio:
- Maximum 32 CPUs per GPU.
- This is a scheduler-level constraint.
- Users cannot request arbitrary CPU counts independent of GPU count.

Therefore:
- 1 GPU → maximum 32 CPUs.
- To request ~76 CPUs, the user must request 3 GPUs (32 × 3 = 96 CPUs maximum available).

The scheduler will not allow 76 CPUs with only 1 GPU.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Explain enforced policy
   - Ampere nodes allocate CPUs in proportion to GPUs.
   - 32 CPUs per GPU maximum.
   - This prevents GPU starvation and ensures fair resource use.

2) Calculate requirement
   - 76 CPUs ÷ 32 CPUs per GPU ≈ 2.38 GPUs.
   - Since GPUs are indivisible, user must request 3 GPUs.

3) Provide correct submission guidance
   Example directives:
     #SBATCH --gres=gpu:3
     #SBATCH --cpus-per-task=76
   (Ensure CPUs requested do not exceed 32 × GPUs requested.)

4) Clarify limitations
   - Cannot exceed enforced CPU-per-GPU ratio.
   - Cannot request fractional GPUs.
   - Cannot override scheduler policy.

5) Do NOT:
   - Suggest bypassing CPU limits.
   - Recommend modifying system configuration.
   - Escalate unless a policy exception is formally requested.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Which Wilkes partition are you using?
- Are you using --ntasks or --cpus-per-task?
- Do you require 76 CPUs per task or across tasks?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: CPU request on Wilkes

Hello Mel,

On Wilkes3 (Ampere), Slurm enforces a maximum of 32 CPUs per GPU to avoid starving GPUs of CPU resources. This means that if you request 1 GPU, you can receive at most 32 CPUs.

To obtain approximately 76 CPUs, you would need to request 3 GPUs (since 32 × 3 = 96 CPUs available). GPUs must be requested in whole numbers, so fractional allocations are not possible.

For example:

  #SBATCH --gres=gpu:3
  #SBATCH --cpus-per-task=76

Please let us know if you need help adjusting your submission script.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- CPU-per-GPU ratio is scheduler-enforced.
- Do not imply the limit can be overridden.
- Avoid suggesting inefficient resource usage beyond policy.
- Ensure guidance aligns with documented Wilkes3 resource allocation rules.
metrics.response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: HPC / Job Submission
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
To increase the number of CPUs used from 32 to 76 on Wilkes, while keeping 1 node, 1 GPU, and 1 task, you need to adjust the number of CPUs allocated per task [2]. However, the exact method to achieve this is not directly stated in the provided context, and there might be limitations based on the node's specifications and the job submission filter [1].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Check the documentation for the Wilkes cluster to understand the node specifications and any limitations on CPU allocation per task [2].
2. Consider using the Wilkes3 partition, which has nodes with 2 x AMD EPYC 7763 64-Core processors, allowing for more CPU cores [3].
3. Adjust the `--ntasks` flag in your job submission script to increase the number of tasks, which might allow for more CPU usage, but be aware that this could affect your job's behavior [2].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- What is the exact job submission script you are using, including any flags for CPU and GPU allocation?
- Have you checked the documentation for any specific guidelines on CPU allocation for GPU jobs on Wilkes?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
To increase the number of CPUs used from 32 to 76 on Wilkes, you may need to adjust your job submission script. Please check the documentation for the Wilkes cluster and consider using the Wilkes3 partition for more CPU cores. If you have further questions or issues, please provide your job submission script for review.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Be aware of the job submission filter's limitations on CPU allocation per GPU [1].
- Always check the documentation for the specific cluster and partition you are using for guidelines on resource allocation [2][3].

<BEGIN_TICKET>
[TICKET_KEY] HPCSSUP-22949
[STATUS] RESOLVED
[CREATED] 2020-02-06T17:10:12.213+0000
[SUMMARY] CPU cores in gpu jobs

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Dear HPC support,
I'm doing computations on Wilkes2, at the moment with jobs on only one 
GPU each. Since there are 12 core CPUs equiped in each node, do I 
automatically get 3 cpu nodes when booking a job with 1 gpu? I'm using 
the python multiprocessing toolbox to load the next data batch in ram 
while training on the GPU.
Thanks,
Thomas

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0001 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Dear Thomas,
Yes you are forced (by our customised job submission filter) to request 3 cpu cores per GPU. If you try to do something different from this then the submission should fail; if you don't specify cpus at all then this is what you will get. The filter does insist that you specify the number of GPUs per node.
SLURM will then also give 1/4 of the node RAM (i.e. ordinary server memory, not GPU memory) per GPU. If you need more, you will need to increase the fraction of the node you are allocated, up to the full 96GB of host memory, by asking for more of the 4 gpus.
Best regards,
Stuart

Greg

Greg

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RFS / Access Method
Technical Action Required: No (guidance + possible storage conversion request)
Escalation Required: Yes (if user requests NFS-RFS conversion)
Urgency: Low–Medium (workflow clarification)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is attempting to locate an RFS (Research File Share) project under /rfs/project/ on HPC and assumes it should be accessible similarly to RDS. This is incorrect.

Standard RFS storage:
- Is not directly mounted on CSD3/HPC compute or login nodes.
- Is accessed via OS-level mounting from institutional machines (SMB on Windows, SMB/NFS on macOS/Linux).
- Cannot be accessed through the HPC filesystem unless converted to NFS-RFS.

User’s intended workflow:
- Active work on RDS
- Backed-up storage on RFS
- Copy between RDS and RFS

This requires clarification of storage models and possibly a request to convert the RFS project to NFS-RFS.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Clarify storage separation
   Explain that:
   - RDS = high-performance Lustre storage mounted on HPC under /rds
   - RFS = Research File Share (backed-up institutional storage)
   - Standard RFS is not accessible from HPC

2) Answer direct question
   The path does not exist on HPC because RFS is not mounted there.
   It must be accessed via:
   - Windows/macOS mount instructions
   - SMB/NFS mount from a workstation

3) Provide supported HPC-access option
   If user needs RFS accessible from HPC, instruct:
   - Request conversion to NFS-RFS
   - Provide documentation link (RFS on CSD3 – NFS-RFS)
   - Clarify that NFS-RFS allows HPC-side mounting but may not offer RDS-level performance

4) Clarify common workflow
   Typical pattern:
   - Active datasets → RDS
   - Backed-up archival data → RFS or RCS
   - Copy data between systems as required
   - NFS-RFS if active + backed-up + HPC access needed

5) If user confirms NFS-RFS is desired
   - Ask them to email support@hpc.cam.ac.uk with:
       Project name
       Project ID
       Confirmation they want RFS converted to NFS-RFS
   - Escalate to storage team

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Do you require the RFS space to be mounted directly on HPC?
- Is this for active HPC workflows or archival/backup only?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: Path to RFS storage

Hello Heather,

RFS storage is not directly mounted on the HPC filesystem, which is why you cannot find it under /rfs/project/.

Standard RFS is accessed by mounting it from your local machine (e.g. via SMB). It is not available on CSD3 login or compute nodes by default.

If you require your RFS project to be accessible from HPC, you can request that it be converted to NFS-RFS. This allows it to be mounted on CSD3, although performance characteristics differ from RDS.

Please see:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/storage/rfs/accessing-rfs/rfs-nfs.html

If you would like to proceed with conversion to NFS-RFS, please email support@hpc.cam.ac.uk with the project name and ID and we can arrange this for you.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not imply RFS is mounted on HPC by default.
- Do not suggest manual mounting inside compute jobs.
- Do not guarantee performance equivalence between RDS and NFS-RFS.
- Escalate only if conversion request is explicitly confirmed.

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: MPI / Slurm Environment / Dawn (PVC9)
Technical Action Required: No (user-side environment correction)
Escalation Required: No (unless issue persists after environment fix)
Urgency: Medium (job fails in batch mode)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user reports an MPI bootstrap failure when running:

    mpirun -np 1 gmx_mpi -h

within a batch job on the pvc9 partition.

Error excerpt:
    HYD_spawn ... execvp error on file /usr/bin/srun (No such file or directory)

Key observations:
- The job works correctly in an interactive session launched via `srun`.
- The batch script manually purges modules and loads a custom module path.
- The error indicates Intel MPI’s Hydra bootstrap cannot locate `srun`.

Most likely cause:
The batch script is not loading the correct Dawn node environment (RHEL9 default stack), so Slurm integration and related paths (including srun) are not correctly initialised inside the job environment.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Ensure correct base environment is loaded
   Instruct user to add:

       module load rhel9/default-dawn

   immediately after `module purge` in their submission script.

   This ensures:
   - Correct RHEL9 runtime environment
   - Proper Slurm integration
   - Required system paths (including srun)
   - Compatible MPI environment

2) Recommend simplified module order
   Example structure:

       module purge
       module load rhel9/default-dawn
       module use /usr/local/dawn/software/spack-rocky9/spack-modules/dawn-2025-03-23/linux-rocky9-sapphirerapids
       module load gromacs/2024.5

   Avoid mixing incompatible stacks unless required.

3) Retest with minimal command
   After modification, test again with:

       mpirun -np 1 gmx_mpi -h

4) If issue persists, request:
   - Full submission script
   - Complete stdout and stderr files
   - Output of:
         which mpirun
         which srun
         module list

EXPECTED SIGNAL AFTER FIX
- No Hydra bootstrap error.
- mpirun executes successfully.
- gmx_mpi help output prints normally.

BRANCH: If still failing
- Confirm Intel MPI is correctly integrated with Slurm.
- Verify that `srun` exists in PATH within job:
      echo $PATH
      ls -l /usr/bin/srun
- Escalate to platform admins only if Slurm binary is genuinely unavailable on compute node (unlikely).

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: MPI-related runtime error on pvc9

Hello Wei-Tse,

It looks like the Dawn node environment is not being fully initialised in your batch job.

Please add the following line after `module purge` in your submission script:

  module load rhel9/default-dawn

This loads the correct RHEL9 runtime and Slurm integration required for MPI jobs on pvc9.

After adding this, please resubmit the job and let us know if the issue persists. If so, please send the full submission script and output files.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not suggest installing MPI manually.
- Do not recommend modifying system Slurm paths.
- Avoid blaming firewall or host availability unless independently verified.
- Keep troubleshooting limited to supported module stacks.

Portal Action Required: No [1][2][3][4]
Urgency: Medium [1][2][3][4]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing an MPI-related runtime error when attempting to use the gmx_mpi executable from the gromacs/2024.5 module on the pvc9 partition [1]. The error message suggests that there is an issue with the bootstrap proxies, and possible reasons include host availability, firewall issues, and slurm bootstrap problems [1]. Similar issues have been reported in the past, and solutions have included loading different MPI modules, adjusting the number of tasks and nodes, and using alternative launchers [2][3][4].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Check the availability of the hosts and the firewall settings [1].
2. Try loading a different MPI module, such as intel-oneapi-mpi [2].
3. Adjust the number of tasks and nodes in the job submission script [3].
4. Consider using an alternative launcher, such as mpirun with the -bootstrap option [1].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide more details about the job submission script and the environment modules loaded?
- Have you tried running the job on a different partition or with a different number of nodes and tasks?

Best regards,
Wei-Tse Hsu

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- When using MPI, it is essential to ensure that the hosts are available and the firewall settings are correct [1].
- Loading different MPI modules or adjusting the number of tasks and nodes may affect the performance and efficiency of the job [2][3][4].

reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: MPI / Slurm Environment / Dawn (PVC9)
Technical Action Required: No (user-side environment correction)
Escalation Required: No (unless issue persists after environment fix)
Urgency: Medium (job fails in batch mode)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user reports an MPI bootstrap failure when running:

    mpirun -np 1 gmx_mpi -h

within a batch job on the pvc9 partition.

Error excerpt:
    HYD_spawn ... execvp error on file /usr/bin/srun (No such file or directory)

Key observations:
- The job works correctly in an interactive session launched via `srun`.
- The batch script manually purges modules and loads a custom module path.
- The error indicates Intel MPI’s Hydra bootstrap cannot locate `srun`.

Most likely cause:
The batch script is not loading the correct Dawn node environment (RHEL9 default stack), so Slurm integration and related paths (including srun) are not correctly initialised inside the job environment.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Ensure correct base environment is loaded
   Instruct user to add:

       module load rhel9/default-dawn

   immediately after `module purge` in their submission script.

   This ensures:
   - Correct RHEL9 runtime environment
   - Proper Slurm integration
   - Required system paths (including srun)
   - Compatible MPI environment

2) Recommend simplified module order
   Example structure:

       module purge
       module load rhel9/default-dawn
       module use /usr/local/dawn/software/spack-rocky9/spack-modules/dawn-2025-03-23/linux-rocky9-sapphirerapids
       module load gromacs/2024.5

   Avoid mixing incompatible stacks unless required.

3) Retest with minimal command
   After modification, test again with:

       mpirun -np 1 gmx_mpi -h

4) If issue persists, request:
   - Full submission script
   - Complete stdout and stderr files
   - Output of:
         which mpirun
         which srun
         module list

EXPECTED SIGNAL AFTER FIX
- No Hydra bootstrap error.
- mpirun executes successfully.
- gmx_mpi help output prints normally.

BRANCH: If still failing
- Confirm Intel MPI is correctly integrated with Slurm.
- Verify that `srun` exists in PATH within job:
      echo $PATH
      ls -l /usr/bin/srun
- Escalate to platform admins only if Slurm binary is genuinely unavailable on compute node (unlikely).

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: MPI-related runtime error on pvc9

Hello Wei-Tse,

It looks like the Dawn node environment is not being fully initialised in your batch job.

Please add the following line after `module purge` in your submission script:

  module load rhel9/default-dawn

This loads the correct RHEL9 runtime and Slurm integration required for MPI jobs on pvc9.

After adding this, please resubmit the job and let us know if the issue persists. If so, please send the full submission script and output files.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not suggest installing MPI manually.
- Do not recommend modifying system Slurm paths.
- Avoid blaming firewall or host availability unless independently verified.
- Keep troubleshooting limited to supported module stacks.

CLASSIFICATION
Category: MPI / GROMACS [1][2][3][4]
Technical Action Required: Yes [1][2][3][4]
Portal Action Required: No [1][2][3][4]
Urgency: Medium [1][2][3][4]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing an MPI-related runtime error when attempting to use the gmx_mpi executable from the gromacs/2024.5 module on the pvc9 partition [1]. The error message suggests that there is an issue with the bootstrap proxies, and possible reasons include host availability, firewall issues, and slurm bootstrap problems [1]. Similar issues have been reported in the past, and solutions have included loading different MPI modules, adjusting the number of tasks and nodes, and using alternative launchers [2][3][4].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Check the availability of the hosts and the firewall settings [1].
2. Try loading a different MPI module, such as intel-oneapi-mpi [2].
3. Adjust the number of tasks and nodes in the job submission script [3].
4. Consider using an alternative launcher, such as mpirun with the -bootstrap option [1].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide more details about the job submission script and the environment modules loaded?
- Have you tried running the job on a different partition or with a different number of nodes and tasks?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
Thank you for your assistance. I will try adjusting the number of tasks and nodes in the job submission script and loading a different MPI module. If the issue persists, I will provide more details about the job submission script and the environment modules loaded.
Best regards,
Wei-Tse Hsu

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- When using MPI, it is essential to ensure that the hosts are available and the firewall settings are correct [1].
- Loading different MPI modules or adjusting the number of tasks and nodes may affect the performance and efficiency of the job [2][3][4].

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RDS / Quota Diagnosis
Technical Action Required: No (user action required to reduce file count)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium (job failing due to quota)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports `Disk quota exceeded` errors while writing files to an RDS project, despite not obviously expecting the project to be full. On RDS, this can happen either because the space quota is exhausted or because the inode/file-count quota has been reached. From the initial report alone, inode exhaustion is a strong possibility, especially given the shell errors creating temporary files, but `quota` output is needed to confirm whether the problem is space usage or file count.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Confirm which quota has been exhausted
   Ask the user to run:
     quota
   and check the relevant RDS project entry.
   - If the GB column is at or above the limit, this is a space-quota problem.
   - If the files column is at the limit or marked with an asterisk (*), this is an inode/file-count problem.

2) Explain the distinction between storage quota and inode quota
   - Storage quota = total disk space (GB/TB)
   - Inode quota = total number of files
   - Hitting either limit blocks new file creation

3) Provide remediation options

   Option A — Reduce file count (recommended if inode limit reached)
   - Delete unnecessary small files
   - Consolidate many small files into archives (e.g. tar)
   - Combine image files into fewer container formats if possible

   Example:
     tar -czf archive_episodes_12000_12100.tar.gz episode_0120*

   Option B — Increase storage allocation
   - Inode quota increases only when total RDS storage allocation increases.
   - This requires purchasing additional storage capacity.
   - Refer to RDS quota documentation.

4) Clarify policy
   - Inode quotas are fixed per TiB.
   - They cannot be increased independently of storage allocation.

5) Close once the user understands which limit was hit and how to proceed.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Subject: Re: Quota exceeded on RDS project

Hello Liyou,

The `Disk quota exceeded` message can be caused either by running out of space or by reaching the inode/file-count limit on the RDS project.

Please run:

  quota

and send us the output for the affected RDS project. If the limit has been hit in the `files` column rather than the GB column, that would indicate an inode quota issue rather than lack of storage space.

If it is an inode limit, the usual remedies are to remove or consolidate many small files or to increase the total storage allocation.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not imply a filesystem fault without first confirming whether space or inode quota has been reached.
- Do not suggest modifying system quota settings directly.
- Inode quotas are proportional to purchased storage and are not adjustable independently.
- Avoid recommending unsafe bulk deletion commands.

#!
#! Example SLURM job script for Wilkes3 (AMD EPYC 7763, ConnectX-6, A100)

#! How many whole nodes should be allocated?
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#! How many (MPI) tasks will there be in total?
#! Note probably this should not exceed the total number of GPUs in use.
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#! Specify the number of GPUs per node (between 1 and 4; must be 4 if nodes>1).
#! Note that the job submission script will enforce no more than 32 cpus per GPU.
#SBATCH --gres=gpu:1

#SBATCH --time=36:00:00

#SBATCH --no-requeue
#! Do not change:

#! sbatch directives end here (put any additional directives above this line)
#! Notes:
#! Charging is determined by GPU number*walltime.
#! Number of nodes and tasks per node allocated by SLURM (do not change):

numtasks=$SLURM_NTASKS

#! Insert additional module load commands after this line if needed:

nvidia-smi
application="./convert.sh"
#! Run options for the application:
options=""
#! Work directory (i.e. where the job will run):
workdir="$SLURM_SUBMIT_DIR" # The value of SLURM_SUBMIT_DIR sets workdir to the directory
1. in which sbatch is run.
#! Are you using OpenMP (NB this is unrelated to OpenMPI)? If so increase this

export OMP_NUM_THREADS=1
#! Number of MPI tasks to be started by the application per node and in total (do not change):
np=$[${numnodes}*${mpi_tasks_per_node}]
#! Choose this for a pure shared-memory OpenMP parallel program on a single node:
#! (OMP_NUM_THREADS threads will be created):

JOBID=$SLURM_JOB_ID

reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RDS / Quota Diagnosis
Technical Action Required: No (user action required to reduce file count)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium (job failing due to quota)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports `Disk quota exceeded` errors while writing files to an RDS project, despite not obviously expecting the project to be full. On RDS, this can happen either because the space quota is exhausted or because the inode/file-count quota has been reached. From the initial report alone, inode exhaustion is a strong possibility, especially given the shell errors creating temporary files, but `quota` output is needed to confirm whether the problem is space usage or file count.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Confirm which quota has been exhausted
   Ask the user to run:
     quota
   and check the relevant RDS project entry.
   - If the GB column is at or above the limit, this is a space-quota problem.
   - If the files column is at the limit or marked with an asterisk (*), this is an inode/file-count problem.

2) Explain the distinction between storage quota and inode quota
   - Storage quota = total disk space (GB/TB)
   - Inode quota = total number of files
   - Hitting either limit blocks new file creation

3) Provide remediation options

   Option A — Reduce file count (recommended if inode limit reached)
   - Delete unnecessary small files
   - Consolidate many small files into archives (e.g. tar)
   - Combine image files into fewer container formats if possible

   Example:
     tar -czf archive_episodes_12000_12100.tar.gz episode_0120*

   Option B — Increase storage allocation
   - Inode quota increases only when total RDS storage allocation increases.
   - This requires purchasing additional storage capacity.
   - Refer to RDS quota documentation.

4) Clarify policy
   - Inode quotas are fixed per TiB.
   - They cannot be increased independently of storage allocation.

5) Close once the user understands which limit was hit and how to proceed.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Subject: Re: Quota exceeded on RDS project

Hello Liyou,

The `Disk quota exceeded` message can be caused either by running out of space or by reaching the inode/file-count limit on the RDS project.

Please run:

  quota

and send us the output for the affected RDS project. If the limit has been hit in the `files` column rather than the GB column, that would indicate an inode quota issue rather than lack of storage space.

If it is an inode limit, the usual remedies are to remove or consolidate many small files or to increase the total storage allocation.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not imply a filesystem fault without first confirming whether space or inode quota has been reached.
- Do not suggest modifying system quota settings directly.
- Inode quotas are proportional to purchased storage and are not adjustable independently.
- Avoid recommending unsafe bulk deletion commands.

CLASSIFICATION
Category: Procurement / Pricing Information
Technical HPC Action Required: No
Finance Action Required: No (information only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is requesting a formal quote for:
- Cumulus CPU 200,000 hours at £0.01/CPU hour
- Research Data Store (RDS) 10 TB for 3 years at £162/TB

HPC Support does not issue formal quotes. Pricing information is published and departments must raise purchase orders via their own finance processes.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Clarify that formal quotes are not issued.
2) Confirm the published pricing model (if appropriate).
3) Advise that departmental finance teams should proceed using the published rates.
4) Do not generate bespoke quotation documents.
5) Do not raise purchase orders on behalf of departments.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: Quote request for Cumulus CPU and RDS

Dear Dave,

Thank you for your message.

We do not issue formal quotation documents for Cumulus CPU time or RDS storage. The services are charged according to the published pricing, and your departmental finance team can proceed based on those rates.

For reference:
- Cumulus CPU usage is charged per CPU hour at the published rate.
- RDS storage is charged per TB per year according to the current pricing schedule.

If your department requires confirmation of the published pricing, this is available on the HPC documentation pages.

Please let us know if you have any further questions.

Best regards,  
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not generate unofficial quote documents.
- Do not commit to pricing beyond the published rates.
- Do not raise purchase orders on behalf of users.
- Ensure any pricing referenced aligns with the current published documentation.

metrics.user_input:
Dear HPC Team,
Could we add a few more recent CUDA modules to the cluster? At the moment I can load cuda/12.1, but many current DL packages (e.g., recent PyTorch/TensorFlow wheels) target CUDA 12.4 or 12.6. Having cuda/12.4 and cuda/12.6available would greatly improve compatibility and avoid building from source.
If feasible, installing these alongside the existing cuda/12.1 (plus matching cuDNN where applicable) would be ideal. I’m happy to test the new modules once they’re deployed.
Many thanks!
Best regards,
Yijiang Dong

benchmark_annotation.summary: Gaia Arcus cloud ceph connection issues

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / CephFS / MDS connectivity (Arcus cloud)
Technical Action Required: Yes (service-side investigation/mitigation)
Escalation Required: Yes (Ceph/storage administrators + network)
Urgency: High (filesystem hangs across multiple instances)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports Ceph shares on Arcus cloud hanging across multiple instances, with client session state showing repeated MDS reconnecting behaviour and filesystem access hanging. Because the issue affects multiple shares and instances, this is more consistent with a service-side CephFS/MDS or network-path problem than a single-instance misconfiguration. Rebooting instances may temporarily clear symptoms but should not be treated as the primary fix.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Acknowledge and treat as a service incident
   - Confirm the issue affects multiple instances/shares.
   - Prioritise service-side checks over detailed user-side debugging.

2) Collect minimal high-signal diagnostics from the user
   Ask for:
   - example affected instance name(s)
   - approximate timestamps of failures
   - exact mount command(s) or error output if readily available

3) Service-side investigation (storage/admin)
   Check:
   - Ceph cluster health summary and recent warnings
   - CephFS filesystem status and active/standby MDS state
   - MDS daemon logs around the failure window
   - client session reconnect/eviction behaviour
   - any ongoing rebalancing or recovery that could affect metadata availability

4) Network path verification (admin)
   Verify routing/interface configuration and any recent changes affecting CephFS traffic.

5) Ask users to retry only after backend checks or remediation have been completed.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Patrick,

Thanks for the detailed report. Because the hangs are affecting multiple instances and shares, this looks more like a service-side CephFS issue than a single-instance configuration problem.

We’re investigating the backend now. In the meantime, could you please share:
- the names of one or two affected instances,
- the approximate timestamps of the most recent failures,
- and one example of the mount command or error output you are seeing?

Once we have checked the backend state, we can advise whether any instance-side action is still needed.

Cheers,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Treat multi-instance CephFS hangs as a service incident; avoid shifting burden to users.
- Avoid making claims about root cause unless supported by service-side evidence.
- Suggest disruptive workarounds (instance reboot) only with explicit user confirmation and after backend stability is restored.
- Do not request or expose sensitive infrastructure details in user-facing messages.
metrics.response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / CephFS Connection Issues
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues with CephFS connections on the Arcus cloud, with commands hanging and awaiting response [1][2]. The mds_sessions file shows a mixture of "reconnecting", "open", and "opening" states [1]. This issue has recurred multiple times, with previous resolutions involving MDS failovers [1][2].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Investigate the current state of the MDS cluster and check for any errors or issues [1].
2. Attempt to fail over the MDS again to see if it resolves the issue [1][2].
3. Review the Ceph logging to try to identify the underlying cause of the problem [1][2].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide more details about the current state of the CephFS connections and the exact error messages you are seeing?
- Have there been any recent changes to the Ceph configuration or the Arcus cloud environment?
- Are all CephFS shares affected, or only specific ones?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
We are currently investigating the issue with CephFS connections on the Arcus cloud. We will attempt to fail over the MDS and review the Ceph logging to identify the cause of the problem. Please let us know if you have any further information or if the issue persists after our initial investigation.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- MDS failovers should be done with caution and only when necessary, as they can cause temporary disruptions to CephFS connections [1][2].
- It is essential to investigate and identify the underlying cause of the issue to prevent future recurrences [1][2].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-86663
[2] : HPCSSUP-87130

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: CORE Procurement / Quote Clarification
Technical Action Required: No (quote preparation and clarification)
Finance Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is requesting pricing for 10,000 additional GPU hours and additional RDS storage on `QAKG-SL2-GPU`. Because the message says `20 GB`, which is unusually small in this procurement context, the safest response is to confirm the intended storage amount and whether the quote should cover only new storage or also any existing storage renewal/extension before issuing a final figure.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Clarify the storage request before quoting
   - Confirm whether `20 GB` is correct or a typo.
   - Confirm whether the user means additional capacity on top of the current licensed storage.
   - Confirm whether the quote should cover only extra storage or also renewal/extension of existing storage.

2) Quote the GPU hours separately using the current applicable rate.

3) Once the storage details are confirmed, issue a written email quote covering both the GPU hours and the corrected storage amount.

4) Ask the user to send the PO to the ticket once ready.

5) Keep any later account-creation request separate from the pricing calculation.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Dear Wenyu,

Before I finalise the storage part of the quote, could you please confirm whether `20 GB` is correct? That is unusually small for this service, so I want to make sure we quote the right storage amount and whether this is additional capacity or an extension of your current storage.

Once you confirm the intended storage quantity and scope, I can provide the full written quote for both storage and GPU hours.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not silently convert user-supplied units.
- If the storage quantity looks implausible, ask before quoting.
- Keep quote assumptions explicit, especially if renewal or extension of existing storage affects cost.

benchmark_annotation.summary: Request for ~/rfs Symlink in Home Directory

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Filesystem / Symlink / Home Quota
Technical Action Required: No (user guidance only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User wants a convenience symlink from `~/rfs` to `/rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw`. The correct command is straightforward, but the ticket shows the attempt failed because the user's `/home` directory was already over quota. In that case the symlink itself cannot be created until home usage is reduced.

ACTION
1) Ask the user to create the link with the concrete command:
   - `cd $HOME`
   - `ln -s /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw $HOME/rfs`
2) If they see `No space left on device`, ask for `quota` output.
3) Explain that the immediate problem is `/home` usage above the 50 GB quota, not an RFS permission problem.
4) Advise them to remove unneeded files from `/home` or move suitable data to `hpc-work`.
5) Mention that accidentally deleted files from `/home` may be recoverable from `~/.snapshot`.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Hansheng,

Please try:

  cd $HOME
  ln -s /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw $HOME/rfs

If you receive `No space left on device`, please run `quota` and send us the output. That would usually indicate your `/home` directory is full, which would prevent creation of even a small symlink entry.

If your home usage is over quota, you will need to move some data into `hpc-work` or delete unwanted files from `/home` before retrying. If you remove anything from `/home` by mistake, you can usually restore it from `~/.snapshot`.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Use the concrete path from the user request when it is already known.
- Distinguish `/home` quota exhaustion from RFS access problems.
- Do not advise deleting RFS data to fix a `/home` quota issue.

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Build/Compiler Toolchain / MPI Fortran Modules
Technical Action Required: Yes (workaround instruction)
Escalation Required: Optional (packaging improvement)
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
`gfortran 13.3` with Intel MPI hit `mpi.mod` read error (`Unexpected EOF`) because Intel MPI Fortran module files in that build were available only for older gfortran ABI versions. Workaround: explicitly include compatible `gfortran/11.1.0` (or `10.2.0`) MPI module path.

ACTION
1) Confirm exact module environment and minimal repro.
2) Explain ABI/module version mismatch.
3) Provide compile workaround with explicit include path.
4) Confirm successful compile with user.
5) Track packaging improvement for future stack.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hi Michael,

Please try compiling with an explicit MPI Fortran module include path:

mpif90 -I /usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/intel-oneapi-mpi-2021.12.1-cvatknvvaxe4ohzbsri6keicyjoy3w7f/mpi/2021.12/include/mpi/gfortran/11.1.0 -c -DMPI precision.F90

(Using `.../gfortran/10.2.0` is also a valid workaround.)

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Be explicit this is a compatibility workaround, not ideal long-term packaging.

Dear Frederic,
I know I said that things now were ok, but the saga is not over 🙁
After fixing my sbatch script, running with omp+mpi was still much much slower than running only with mpi. I remembered that there is a knows issue about ifort and omp, so I did a test on cclake: I compiled my code (without mpi) using gfortran (11.2) and ifort (2019.4), and run simply with -omp 28. What took ifort 10 minutes, took gfortran 10 seconds!
So, for my purpose, I need to compile with gfortran. I tried this on icelake with:
module purge
module load rhel8/cclake/base
module load gcc/11
module load intel-oneapi-mpi/2021.12.1/gcc/
Then ran cmake:
The Fortran compiler found was:  /usr/local/software/spack/archive2/linux-scientific7-skylake_avx512/gcc-9.3.0/gcc-11.2.0-do3vmb6iub2hwfop75wwnhbsk3s2ucpi/bin/gfortran
Mpi Fortran found was: /usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/intel-oneapi-mpi-2021.12.1-cvatknvvaxe4ohzbsri6keicyjoy3w7f/mpi/2021.12/lib/libmpifort.so (found version "3.1")
Then I compiled but got the same error as at the beginning:
f951: Fatal Error: Reading module '/usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/intel-oneapi-mpi-2021.12.1-cvatknvvaxe4ohzbsri6keicyjoy3w7f/mpi/2021.12/include/mpi/mpi_f08.mod' at line 1 column 2: Unexpected EOF
compilation terminated.
make[2]: *** [CMakeFiles/scone.dir/build.make:309: CMakeFiles/scone.dir/SharedModules/mpi_func.f90.o] Error 1
make[1]: *** [CMakeFiles/Makefile2:789: CMakeFiles/scone.dir/all] Error 2
make: *** [Makefile:91: all] Error 2
I believe that when trying this on cclake, the code can't find mpi Fortran at all.
Any ideas? Compiling with gfortan + intel MPI now seems like the only option for me.
Best,
Valeria

/home/mjr19/hello

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0001 role=OTHER>
@Frederic Brochu, it might be worth clarifying that the SLURM `--cpus-per-task` option controls the number of CPUs allocated to each MPI task by SLURM and `OMP_NUM_THREADS`, controls the number of threads used by the OpenMP runtime so both are necessary. Typically, I set `--cpus-per-task` manually and then do
```
export OMP_NUM_THREADS=SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK
```
further down in my submission script.
</MESSAGE>

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Job Execution / MPI under Slurm
Technical Action Required: No (user guidance with documented launch pattern)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User attempted direct `mpirun` and saw MPI startup/initialisation errors. For this environment, MPI jobs should be run inside a Slurm allocation using the documented submission pattern. Running directly from a login shell is a common cause of PMPI/launcher errors.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Confirm launch context
   - Ask whether command was run:
     - directly on a login node, or
     - inside a Slurm job allocation.

2) Provide documented MPI job pattern
   - Instruct user to submit via `sbatch` with account/partition/resources set.
   - Recommend launching tasks with `srun` inside the job script.

3) Provide minimal working template
```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH -A <ACCOUNT>
#SBATCH -p <PARTITION>
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --time=00:10:00

module purge
module load <required-modules>

srun ~/meme-mpi/bin/meme --help
```

4) If user needs interactive testing
   - First allocate resources interactively (documented method), then run with `srun` in that allocation.

5) Reference documentation
   - Share the MPI jobs section for the relevant partition.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Milena,

This error usually occurs when MPI is launched outside a proper Slurm allocation.

Please run your MPI command through a Slurm job script, for example:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH -A <ACCOUNT>
#SBATCH -p <PARTITION>
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --time=00:10:00

module purge
module load <required-modules>

srun ~/meme-mpi/bin/meme --help
```

Submit with:

`sbatch <script_name>.sh`

Please also refer to:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/a100.html?highlight=mpirun#jobs-requiring-mpi

Using the documented Slurm launcher pattern should resolve this startup issue.

Regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Avoid ad-hoc launcher advice that bypasses Slurm resource allocation.
- Keep examples aligned with documented partition/account usage.
metrics.response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: MPI / Job Execution [1]
Technical Action Required: Yes [1]
Portal Action Required: No [1]
Urgency: Medium [1]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues with running MPI-enabled processes on nodes, with an error message indicating a fatal error in PMPI_Init_thread [1]. This issue has been encountered by other users in the past, with similar error messages [2][3][4]. The cause of the issue is likely related to the MPI implementation or configuration [1][2].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
To resolve this issue, the user should try loading the OpenMPI module instead of Intel MPI [4]. The correct OpenMPI module for Icelake nodes is openmpi/4.1.3/intel/iwzoswji [4]. The user should purge all modules, load the default Icelake module, and then load the OpenMPI module [4]. If the issue persists, the user should check the documentation for their specific MPI implementation and software requirements [1][2][3].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide more information about your job submission script and the output file?
- Have you tried running your code on a different node or partition?
- Are there any specific software or library requirements for your code?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
To troubleshoot the MPI issue, please try loading the OpenMPI module and running your job again. If the issue persists, please provide more information about your job submission script and output file.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- MPI implementations and configurations can vary depending on the node and partition [1][2].
- It is recommended to check the documentation for specific MPI implementations and software requirements [1][2][3].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-61542
[2] : HPCSSUP-76564
[3] : HPCSSUP-52098
[4] : HPCSSUP-51479

Best regards 
Namu Kroupa

MPIR_pmi_init(167)...: PMI2_Job_GetId returned 14 
I think this is because mpi4py doesn't work with the version of MPI installed, see [https://stackoverflow.com/questions/38985018/mpi4py-only-works-under-mpiexec](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/38985018/mpi4py-only-works-under-mpiexec). 
Could you please let me know which version of MPI works? I ran this on one of the icelake machines (login-q-2). 
Best regards 
Namu Kroupa

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Access / RCS Connectivity / SSH-MFA
Technical Action Required: No (user onboarding guidance)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports that multiple people are unable to connect to `rcs.uis.cam.ac.uk` over SFTP/SSH, raising the possibility of either a broader service problem or account-specific access changes. The correct first response is to confirm whether there is an active service incident and, if not, gather examples from affected users to determine whether the problem is shared or user-specific.

ACTION
1) Check whether there is an active service issue affecting RCS SSH/SFTP access.
2) Ask for example usernames, client/method used, and the exact error messages seen by affected users.
3) If the service is healthy, distinguish between users who cannot access RCS at all and users with account-specific denial errors.
4) For individual users, confirm SSH/storage MFA enrolment if relevant and ask them to raise their own tickets for account-specific checks.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Shaun,

We’re first checking whether there is any broader issue affecting RCS SSH/SFTP access.

If the service itself is healthy, we’ll need the affected usernames, the client or method being used, and the exact error messages so we can tell whether this is a shared problem or an account-specific one.

For users who can access the web portal but not SSH/SFTP services, we may also need to confirm that SSH/storage MFA has been set up separately.

If any individual user continues to have problems, please ask them to raise their own ticket so we can check their account details directly.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not troubleshoot third-party user accounts through one reporter without user-specific consent/context.
metrics.response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Connection Issue [1][2][3][4][5]
Technical Action Required: Yes [1][2][3][4][5]
Portal Action Required: No [1][2][3][4][5]
Urgency: Medium [1][2][3][4][5]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
There have been previous instances of connection issues with the RCS, including errors such as "Connection closed by remote host" [2], "No route to host" [5], and issues with rsync and sftp connections [4]. The causes of these issues have varied, including problems with the sftp gateways [5] and network connectivity [5]. Given the current error message and the fact that multiple users are experiencing the issue, it is likely that there is a technical problem that needs to be addressed [1][2][3][4][5].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Investigate the current status of the RCS and its connectivity [1][2][3][4][5].
2. Check the logs for any error messages or indications of what might be causing the issue [2][3][4].
3. If necessary, escalate the issue to the storage team for further investigation and resolution [1][2][3][4][5].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide more details about the error message you are receiving and the steps you have taken so far to troubleshoot the issue?
- Have you tried connecting to the RCS using a different method, such as rsync or sftp?
- Are there any other users in your department who are experiencing similar issues?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
Thank you for reaching out to us about the issue with connecting to the RCS. We apologize for the inconvenience this has caused and are working to resolve the issue as quickly as possible. In the meantime, please try connecting using a different method, such as rsync or sftp. If you continue to experience issues, please let us know and we will escalate the issue to our storage team for further investigation.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- When investigating connection issues, it is essential to check the logs and error messages to determine the cause of the problem [2][3][4].
- The storage team should be notified and involved in the investigation and resolution of the issue if necessary [1][2][3][4][5].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-29314
[2] : HPCSSUP-89893
[3] : HPCSSUP-91273
[4] : HPCSSUP-88524
[5] : HPCSSUP-91241

This was caused by keepalived deciding to drop its internal routing IP. Packets could come in, but wouldn’t come out, functionally bottlenecking the sftp gateways.
Annoyingly, it left no notification of this happening, or any record of the change, so I can’t tell when this occurred or why.

I’ll have to rebuild these machines soon anyway - they’re EL7.

Anyway - this should be fixed now. My external connections to [rcs.hpc.cam.ac.uk](http://rcs.hpc.cam.ac.uk) work, which is an alias for the same DNS entry as [rcs.uis.cam.ac.uk](http://rcs.uis.cam.ac.uk) .

What could be the source of the error?

Niek

User on VPN is unable to log in via the Citrix Workspace app. The appropriate first diagnostic is to verify whether browser-based launch through `anywhere.cam.ac.uk` works and whether the SRCP desktop can be started there, which helps separate a Citrix client problem from a broader access issue.

https://anywhere.cam.ac.uk/

HPC Support

- Do not assume Citrix backend fault without basic path validation.

<MESSAGE id=0009 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hello Eden,
Looking at the Apple website, MacOS 11 (Big Sur) is no longer supported by them. This means your machine won’t have been getting security patches, etc. over the last year or so and can’t run the latest version of the Citrix Workspace app, that manages the connection to the SRCP platform.
The SRCP platform itself gets regular updates, so it may be that the version of the Citrix app on your machine is now too old to securely connect. Alternatively, the problem could be something different, in which case a reset of the Citrix App and setting the connection up again may fix this. The Citrix website has a page on resetting the App at https://docs.citrix.com/en-us/citrix-workspace-app-for-mac/troubleshooting.html#support-for-resetting-citrix-workspace-app - could you try following the steps and see if you can connect after setting up the connection again.
We’d strongly recommend upgrading your version of MacOS to one that is still supported,
...[truncated for evaluator]

User added a DiRAC project in SAFE but it is not yet visible in `mybalance`. Provisioning is not immediate, so the next step is to check whether the account association is still pending and complete the internal provisioning workflow if required.

ACTION
1) Check provisioning status for requested project association.
2) Complete pending provisioning.
3) Once provisioning has completed, confirm that the account is visible and usable.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Richard,

Provisioning for this project is not automatic. We will check whether the project association is still pending on our side and complete the required provisioning step if necessary.

We will update you once that has been checked.

Best regards,

- Do not treat SAFE change as instantly reflected in Slurm/accounting systems.

CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Account Management [1]
Technical Action Required: Yes [1]
Urgency: Medium [1]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user, rab200, has added a new DiRAC project (DP371) to their user account via SAFE but is unable to see the project resources on CSD3/Wilkes, and 'mybalance' does not show the project [1]. This issue is similar to previous cases where users have experienced difficulties with project visibility after being added to a new project in SAFE [2][3][4][5].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify that the user has been successfully added to the DP371 project in SAFE [1].
2. Check if the project DP371 is visible when running 'mybalance' [1].
3. If the project is not visible, add the user to the DP371 group and ensure they have the necessary permissions [4][5].
4. If the issue persists, investigate potential database issues or conflicts with other projects [5].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you confirm that you have requested to join the DP371 project in SAFE and that the request has been approved?
- Have you tried running 'mybalance' on a different node or after refreshing your connection?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
I have checked and I am able to see the DP371 project in SAFE, but it is still not visible when I run 'mybalance'. Please let me know the next steps to resolve this issue.
Best regards,
Richard

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that all actions are taken in accordance with the relevant policies and procedures for managing user accounts and project resources [1][2][3][4][5].

[1] : HPCSSUP-46688
[2] : HPCSSUP-21241
[3] : HPCSSUP-30805
[4] : HPCSSUP-89807
[5] : HPCSSUP-79253

[SUMMARY] DiRAC Director's Discretionary time award

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Dear Vincent, 
  
Thank you for your recent application for DiRAC Director’s Discretionary time, entitled “Composite Dynamics signatures at the LHC”. Your proposal was reviewed by myself, the DiRAC RAC chairs (cc’ed) and members of the appropriate RAC sub-panel. 
  
I am very pleased to inform you that your proposal was highly ranked and you will be allocated 6M core hours on the Cascade Lake cluster in the Data Intensive service at Cambridge. 
  
Please could you confirm that you are still able to make use of this allocation before March 31st 2021.  
  
I have cc’ed the Cambridge technical support team and the Edinburgh DiRAC support team who will work with you to get your allocation set up. In order to access your allocation, you should contact them as soon as possible. A new SAFE project code will be set up for this allocation. 
  
I would be grateful if you can keep me updated on the progress of your project – I will request a formal report in April, but it’s useful for me to know how you are getting on before then. 
  
Congratulations again, and I look forward to seeing the output of your calculations. 
  
Best wishes, 
  
Mark. 
  
---------------------------------------

benchmark_annotation.summary: Setting Up Local Conda Environment for Intel GPU Usage on DAWN Cluster

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software Environment / Dawn PVC / PyTorch-IPEX
Technical Action Required: No (user setup guidance)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User wants a modifiable local environment equivalent to centrally managed `pytorch-gpu`. Central env is immutable. Supported options:
- activate central `pytorch-gpu` then layer user virtualenv, or
- build user conda env with compatible pinned versions from Intel channel and test on PVC nodes.

ACTION
1) Confirm central env baseline works.
2) Explain central env cannot be modified.
3) Provide two supported approaches:
   - `pytorch-gpu` + user virtualenv layering
   - standalone conda env with compatible pins (`intel-extension-for-pytorch`, `pytorch`, python range) and Intel repo
4) Require testing on PVC/PVC9 interactive node.
5) Set required env vars for XPU runtime test.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hi Moritz,

`pytorch-gpu` is centrally managed and cannot be modified directly.

Supported options are:
1) Activate `pytorch-gpu`, then activate your own virtualenv for extra packages.
2) Create your own conda env with compatible versions (e.g. pinned `intel-extension-for-pytorch` + `pytorch`) and test it on PVC nodes.

Please use the Dawn guidance here:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/pvc.html#machine-learning-data-science-frameworks

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not advise modifying centrally managed environments.
metrics.response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: PyTorch Environment Setup [1]
Technical Action Required: Yes [1]
Portal Action Required: No [1]
Urgency: Medium [1]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is trying to create a local Conda environment compatible with the Intel GPU setup used on DAWN, and ensure the installation of all necessary packages without running into access issues [1]. The user has attempted to clone the pytorch-gpu environment and install all its modules manually but encountered HTTP 403 errors [1].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
To create a local Conda environment compatible with the Intel GPU setup used on DAWN, we recommend the following steps:
1. Visit the PyTorch extension website and follow the installation instructions for the Intel GPU [4].
2. Create a fresh Conda environment using `conda create -n pvctest` and activate it [4].
3. Install the required packages using `conda install intel-extension-for-pytorch=2.3.110 pytorch=2.3.1 -c https://software.repos.intel.com/python/conda -c conda-forge` [4].
4. Launch an interactive job on the PVC partition to test the environment [4].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide more details about the HTTP 403 errors you encountered while trying to clone the pytorch-gpu environment?
- Have you tried installing the required packages using the Intel Conda channel?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
To create a local Conda environment compatible with the Intel GPU setup used on DAWN, please follow the steps outlined above. If you encounter any issues, please provide more details about the errors you are seeing.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- When creating a local Conda environment, make sure to use the correct installation instructions and channels to avoid compatibility issues [1][4].
- Be cautious when installing packages from external channels, as they may not be compatible with the DAWN environment [1][4].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-75523
[4] : HPCSSUP-89783
metrics.user_input:
Dear HPC Team,
I am currently using the DAWN cluster and would like to leverage the Intel GPUs for running my PyTorch code.
According to the documentation, the following setup works well:
module load intelpython-conda
conda activate pytorch-gpu
And in Python:
import torch
import intel_extension_for_pytorch as ipex
...
1. Enable GPU
model = model.to('xpu')
data = data.to('xpu')
model = ipex.optimize(model, dtype=torch.float32)
This setup functions as expected.
However, the pytorch-gpu environment appears to be a system-provided environment. For the purposes of my project, I would like to create and manage my own local Conda environment that is compatible with the Intel GPUs (i.e., includes PyTorch and the Intel Extension for PyTorch with xpu support).
I have attempted to clone the pytorch-gpu environment as well as install all of its modules manually, but I run into repeated HTTP 403 errors when trying to fetch packages from the Intel Conda channel. This seems to indicate restricted access or deprecation of some packages or channels.
Could you please advise on the recommended way to:
1.  Create a local Conda environment compatible with the Intel GPU setup used on DAWN,
2.  Ensure installation of all necessary packages without running into access issues?
If there is a supported method to export or replicate the pytorch-gpu environment into a user-managed Conda environment, that would be ideal.
Thank you very much for your assistance!
Best wishes,
Moritz

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Assistance with Cloning and Customizing PyTorch Environment on DAWN

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Hi,
I hope this message finds you well.
I am currently working with PyTorch on the DAWN supercomputer and have encountered a few issues. I noticed there is a pre-made environment named pytorch-gpu available. I loaded it using the following commands:
module load intelpython-conda
conda activate pytorch-gpu
Issue 1: I cannot modify it, even just for my session. I tried to install libraries but it does not work. Would you have an idea why?
Issue 2: I attempted to clone the environment locally to install my own libraries using the command:
conda create --name my-pytorch-gpu --clone pytorch-gpu
However, this approach didn’t work. I also tried specifying the conda-forge channel:
conda create --name my-pytorch-gpu --clone pytorch-gpu -c conda-forge
But same error.
Additionally, if you have any recommendations or guidelines for creating a new environment from scratch, especially considering the XPU setup on DAWN, I would greatly appreciate it. The PyTorch website provides options for CUDA and CPU, but I’m unsure how to configure it for XPU.
If you have any information on how the pytorch-gpu environment was built, that would also be very helpful.
Thank you for your assistance!
Best regards,
Francois Porcher

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Unable to submit jobs on DAWN

Dear Muhammad,
Thanks a lot for the reply. Now I am able to submit my jobs. I do have another quick question if I may: I was trying to use PyTorch with the Intel GPUs on DAWN. I was following instructions here: [https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/pvc.html?highlight=pytorch#pytorch](https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/pvc.html?highlight=pytorch#pytorch)

<MESSAGE id=0001 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hi Tony,
Could you try:
`module purge
module load rhel9/default-dawn
module load intelpython-conda/2025.0

This is the Modules which I commonly use.
SimonH

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0002 role=TICKET_CREATOR>

Tony W

Another quick question.

Tony W

Dear support,

Following the official tutorial at

python
import torch

UserWarning: Failed to load image Python extension: ''If you don't plan

I've been meeting similar errors when trying to create my own Conda

Could you please help me with this problem?
Yours sincerely,
Tony W

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Running GNN on Dawn

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Good afternoon,
I am trying to run a GNN model using Pytorch with GPUs in Dawn. I am having some trouble setting up the python environment with all the appropriate dependencies. Firstly, I tried to create my own python virtual environment, however this is the wrong torch version to use with the intel GPUs.
I then tried to use the conda environment that already exists in the cluster called pytorch-gpu. However, this specific environment is missing other packages that I need. Would I be able to download additional packages into this conda environment (I also tried cloning it but it failed)? Or should I use my own python environment? In that case, would you have any advice on how to download the appropriate torch version, as well as its derived packages, to use with the intel GPUs (torch-geometric, torch-scatter, torch-sparse).
I would also like to know if it would be possible to use Weights and Biases to carry out hyperparameter sweeps and log these results online.
Thank you for your help!
Best wishes,
Clara

<BEGIN_TICKET>
[TICKET_KEY] HPCSSUP-89783
[STATUS] RESOLVED
[CREATED] 2025-06-26T18:57:17.947+0100
[SUMMARY] Running GNN on Dawn

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Good afternoon,
I am trying to run a GNN model using Pytorch with GPUs in Dawn. I am having some trouble setting up the python environment with all the appropriate dependencies. Firstly, I tried to create my own python virtual environment, however this is the wrong torch version to use with the intel GPUs.
I then tried to use the conda environment that already exists in the cluster called pytorch-gpu. However, this specific environment is missing other packages that I need. Would I be able to download additional packages into this conda environment (I also tried cloning it but it failed)? Or should I use my own python environment? In that case, would you have any advice on how to download the appropriate torch version, as well as its derived packages, to use with the intel GPUs (torch-geometric, torch-scatter, torch-sparse).
I would also like to know if it would be possible to use Weights and Biases to carry out hyperparameter sweeps and log these results online.
Thank you for your help!
Best wishes,
Clara

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0001 role=OTHER>
Hi Clara,
Indeed yes the site packages are not writable but not to worry as there are two main options available to us. 
Option 1:
This method assumes you have your own install of mini/ana-conda and are able to create a new venv for this purpose. We shall be installing the same versions of PyTorch as the built in venv.
Start by visiting https://pytorch-extension.intel.com/installation?platform=gpu&version=v2.3.110%2Bxpu&os=linux%2Fwsl2&package=conda Then we will proceed as follows:
1. Create a fresh venv 
- `conda create -n pvctest`
- `conda activate pvctest`
2. Install our Python version
- `conda install 'python>3.10,<3.12'`
- Install the packages `conda install intel-extension-for-pytorch=2.3.110 pytorch=2.3.1 -c https://software.repos.intel.com/python/conda -c conda-forge`
3. Launch an interactive job on PVC partition to test
- Make sure no conflicting modules etc are active (may also require you to run `conda deactivate` a couple of times to make sure nothing is active)
- Activate the venv we just created and run the test function
```
export OCL_ICD_VENDORS=/etc/OpenCL/vendors
export CCL_ROOT=${CONDA_PREFIX}
python -c "import torch; import intel_extension_for_pytorch as ipex; print(torch.__version__); print(ipex.__version__); [print(f'[{i}]: {torch.xpu.get_device_properties(i)}') for i in range(torch.xpu.device_count())];"
```
You will find that since we dropped back to a slightly earlier release of this software, we have Python 3.11.11 which I think should be compatible with the other software you’d like to install on this venv. Note that step 3 will only work on the PVC nodes. This method will also align our install with the centrally available one on the cluster (i.e., there shouldn’t be any compatibility issue(s)). You are then free to install any other packages you require in your personal venv. 
Option 2:
Use the built in conda venv 
 To begin, let’s check that everything works before installing additional packages. The procedure I propose is thus:
1. Deactivate your own conda (this includes commenting it out of and then re-sourcing your bashrc)
2. Grab a pvc node as you have been doing
3. activate `pytorch-gpu` (see https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/pvc.html#pytorch )
4. Create a virtualenv and install your packages in that
The result is that for option 2, you will have to activate both the conda venv and the virtualenv
I will check with my colleagues about your other questions. Thanks.
Best wishes,
Henry
HPC Support
</MESSAGE>

User could not locate AIRR/Dawn storage path in docs. The exact project path is user/project-specific and should be discovered from the user account rather than assumed.

ACTION
1) Ask user to check available RDS links under home:
   - `ls -ld ~/rds/*`
2) Ask user to identify the AIRR project path from quota/project listing:
   - `quota`
3) Confirm the matching path format once identified (typically `~/rds/rds-<project-id>` symlink).
4) Clarify that `~/rds/...` is a convenience link to the underlying project path.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Phil,

Your AIRR storage path is user/project specific. Please run:

`ls -ld ~/rds/*`
`quota`

This will show the project link available in your account (typically `~/rds/rds-<project-id>`).  
That entry is a symlink to the underlying project storage location.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not hardcode project-specific paths unless verified for that user/account.

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Slurm Scheduling Status Interpretation
Technical Action Required: No (explanation + minor script advice)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User saw scheduler reason text and assumed there was a post-maintenance fault. From the script provided, there is no obvious submission syntax problem, and this kind of reason string can still appear while a job is simply pending under normal scheduler priority. Additional optimisation: explicit `--mem` is redundant for cclake in this case.

ACTION
1) Check live job state and reason (`squeue`, `scontrol`).
2) Explain pending-by-priority behavior.
3) Suggest removing redundant `--mem` directive when CPU request already implies desired memory.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Hamish,

From the script you sent, the job appears to have submitted correctly and may simply be pending under normal scheduler priority.

For this script, `--mem=27360M` is redundant if you already request the corresponding CPU count on `cclake`; removing it can simplify scheduling requests.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Distinguish temporary reason strings from real submission errors.
metrics.response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Job Scheduling / Node Availability [1][2]
Technical Action Required: Yes [1][2]
Escalation Required: Possibly [1]
Urgency: Medium [1]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The job is pending with a "ReqNodeNotAvail, Reserved for maintenance" status, despite maintenance having finished last week [1][2]. This suggests that there may be an issue with node availability or scheduling [1][2].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
Check the current node status and availability on the cclake partition [1]. Investigate if there are any ongoing maintenance or issues that may be causing the nodes to be reserved [1][2]. If necessary, consider resubmitting the job to a different partition or adjusting the job script to exclude specific nodes [1][2].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Are there any current or scheduled maintenance activities that may be affecting node availability [1][2]?
- Can you provide more information about the job script and submission process [1]?
- Have you tried resubmitting the job or adjusting the job script to exclude specific nodes [1][2]?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
We are investigating the issue with node availability on the cclake partition. In the meantime, you can try resubmitting the job to a different partition or adjusting the job script to exclude specific nodes. Please let us know if you have any further questions or concerns.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Node availability and scheduling are subject to change due to maintenance and other activities [1][2].
- It is recommended to check the current node status and availability before submitting jobs [1].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-97398
[2] : HPCSSUP-45849
metrics.user_input:
Hi -
I’ve just sbatched a job on the HPC, using cclake, job 9082481_[1-2]. If I squeue -u has27 it tells me (under NODELIST) 'ReqNodeNotAvail, Reserved for maintenance’. But maintenance finished last week.
Can you tell me what (if anything) I’ve done wrong? Script below.
Thanks,
Hamish
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH -A GLOVER-SL3-CPU
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --mail-type=NONE
#SBATCH -p cclake  # name of the partition to run job on
#SBATCH -o /home/has27/rds/hpc-work/f2reads/bowtie2/bowtie2%a.log
#SBATCH -c 8         # number of CPUs. Default: 1
#SBATCH --mem=27360M     # RAM memory.
#SBATCH -t 12:00:00  # time for the job HH:MM:SS. Default: 1 min
#SBATCH -a 1-2
source ~/.bashrc
1. activate conda environment
conda activate bowtie2
cd /home/has27/rds/hpc-work/f2reads/soapnuke/clean
1. List all files in the folder starting with A or B,
2. then get the relevant line
INDIV_ID=$(ls -d [AB]* | head -n ${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID} | tail -n 1)
cd $INDIV_ID
READ1=$(ls *1.fq.gz)
READ2=$(ls *2.fq.gz)
cd /home/has27/rds/hpc-work/Genomes/LSRagtag
bowtie2 -p 8 -x LSRagtag -1 /home/has27/rds/hpc-work/f2reads/soapnuke/clean/$INDIV_ID/$READ1 -2 /home/has27/rds/hpc-work/f2reads/soapnuke/clean/$INDIV_ID/$READ2 | samtools sort > /home/has27/rds/hpc-work/f2reads/align-LSRagtag/$INDIV_ID.sorted.bam
—
Dr Hamish Symington
Junior Research Fellow, Queens’ College, Cambridge
Academic Skills Tutor and Bye-Fellow, Downing College, Cambridge
Postdoctoral Researcher in the Glover Lab, Department of Plant Sciences, University of Cambridge
